license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qxzdxp6
title: Isolation of axenic Symbiodinium cultures

steps_raw.instruction: Several anemones were disrupted using a glass homogenizer so as to leave the algal cells largely intact.

The algal cells were pelleted by centrifugation at 1,000g and then washed by resuspension and repelleting the cells several times in filter-sterilized ASW containing 50µg mL -1 kanamycin,100µg mL -1 ampicillin, and 50µg mL -1 streptomycinantibiotics KAS (Kan, Amp and Strep)
Prepare solid IMK medium: add 1g Agar into 90 mL ASW, and autoclave at 122ºC for 30 minutes.

The algae in liquid solution were then spread onto solid IMK + KAS medium. 1. To be able to have single colonies for later pick up or purification, plating different amount of cells are recommended. 2. Wrap plates in parafilm to prevent evaporation/drying of the medium. 3. Expected amount of time needed for visible colony growth is around 30 days, although some stains may take more or less time.
Then individual colonies were picked with the inoculation loop under microscope and streaked onto solid IMK + KAS medium.
Repeat step 8 procedure for several times until a clean single colony was formed. Those are potentially axenic colnal colonies.
Identify if the colonies are bacteria-free by transfering them to rich solid medium Marine Broth (MB). If there is bacterial or other contaminatin, they will grow on MB plate.

If there is no contamination on MB plate, further verify by PCR using specific bacterial 16s primers. Extract genomic DNA from the colony/cells grown on the plate. PCR reactions contains 0.3 M of each primer in 2X GoTaq Master Mix (Promega, Madison, WI, USA) and were performed by incubating at 94°C for 5 min followed by 30 cycles of 94°C for 30 s, 50°C for 1 min, and 72°C for 1.5 min, with a final incubation at 72°C for 5 min. 16S bacterial primers: first set:63F: 5′-CAGGCCTAACACATGCAAGTC-3′1542R: 5′-AAGGAGGTGATCCAGCCGCA-3′second set:27F: 5′-AGAGTTTGATCCTGGCTCAG-3′1492R: 5′-GGTTACCTTGTTACGACTT-3′

To identify the Symbidonium, PCR using 18S and Chloroplast Cp23S sequences to assign the clades. Detailed protocol can be found in the following link. Primer seqeunces: 18S-F: 5′-GGTTGATCCTGCCAGTAGTCATATGCTTG-3′ 18S-R: 5′-GATCCTTCCGCAGGTTCACCTACGGAAACC-3′ Cp23S-F: 5′-GACGGCTGTAACTATAACGG-3′ Cp23S-R: 5′-CCATCGTATTGAACCCAGC-3′ https://www.protocols.io/view/symbiodinium-cp23s-rflp-and-sequence-analysis-qyjdxun

Further verify the axenic culture by imaging the cells under the microscope. (1) Fix the cells by using 4% PFA for more than one hour in 4ºC; (2) Wash the cells using PBS buffer for 6 times; (3) Stain the cells with DAPI; (4) Examine the cells by bright-field and fluorescence microscopy. If the culture is axenic, bacteria would not be observed in the culture medium or associated with the Symbiodinium cells. Here is an example image of Symbiodinium cell from an axenic culture stained with DAPI. Scale bar: 3 µm

After autoclave, let it cool down to around 65 °C, add sterile 10X Daigo's IMK Medium stock solution as well as KAS antibiotic cocktail: 50µg mL -1 kanamycin,100µg mL -1 ampicillin, and 50µg mL -1 streptomycin.
Prepare a flame-sterilized inoculation loop, allowed to cool to room temperature for use;
